  1. Pre-Trip Planning
  2. Train Travel in Italy

Train Travel in Italy

Trenitalia & Italo Routes

logistics
trains
transportation
Published

June 5, 2026

Overview

Italian trains are efficient, comfortable, and one of the best ways to travel between cities. Our trip involves three major train journeys, all bookable through Trenitalia or Italo (Italy’s two main rail operators).

Key Routes: 1. Florence → Naples (June 10): Italo high-speed #8902, 3 hours — BOOKED (ticket PIDCMA) 2. Naples → Brindisi (June 13): Via flight — AZ 1270 NAP→FCO + AZ 1623 FCO→BDS (see Flights)

Booking Websites

  • Trenitalia.com - Italy’s national rail operator
  • Italotreno.it - Private high-speed operator

Booking Window: 2-4 weeks ahead for best “Super Economy” fares

Payment: Major credit cards accepted, often cheaper to book directly vs. third-party sites

Route 1: Florence → Naples (June 10) — BOOKED

Overview

This is one of Italy’s premier high-speed train routes — fast, comfortable, and scenic.

Booked: Italo #8902, ticket code PIDCMA Departs: Firenze S.M. 12:28pm → Arrives Napoli Centrale 3:28pm Car: Salotto 1 (Lounge) — Seats: Jane (1), Dan (2) Duration: 3 hours even Stations: Firenze SMN → Napoli Centrale

Fare Classes

Class Price Features
Super Economy €20-€50 Advance purchase, limited flexibility
Economy €35-€65 Standard flexibility
Business/Prima €50-€120 More space, quieter car, snacks included

Recommendation: Book Economy or Business class 2-4 weeks ahead for best pricing.

What to Expect

Frecciarossa Features: - Modern, clean trains - Comfortable reclining seats - Power outlets at seats - WiFi (sometimes unreliable) - Quiet cars available - Café/bar car - Luggage storage areas

Seat Selection: - Window seats for views of Italian countryside - Table seats if traveling with companion - Solo seats (1+2 configuration in some cars)

Scenic Highlights

The route passes through: - Tuscan countryside - Apennine mountains - Approaches to Naples with Vesuvius views

Best Views: Sit on the right side (west) approaching Naples for potential Vesuvius sighting.

Stations

Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN) - Central Florence station - Arrive 15-20 minutes early - Multiple platforms (check departure boards) - Luggage storage available

Napoli Centrale - Main Naples station - Can be chaotic - watch belongings - Taxi stand outside - Metro connections - Hotel pickups coordinate here

Route 2: Naples → Brindisi — CHANGED TO FLIGHT (June 13)

Overview

ImportantThis leg is now a flight, not a train

Booked: ITA Airways (AZ), Reservation IXYZMT, departing Saturday June 13

Flight Route Departs Arrives
AZ 1270 NAP → FCO (Rome) 3:05pm 4:05pm
AZ 1623 FCO → BDS (Brindisi) 5:15pm 6:25pm

See Flights and Day 9 for full details.

The train research below is retained for reference (original research notes).

Original planned route: Naples → Brindisi by Intercity train (~5.5 hours direct)

Route Options

Route Train Type Duration Changes Class Fare
Naples → Brindisi (IC) Intercity Express ~5h 30m 0 (Direct) 1st + 2nd €15-€40 (1st)
Naples → Bari Frecciarossa/Italo 3h 15-4h 0-1 Various €20-€60
Naples → Brindisi (old route) Frecce + Regional 5-6h 1 (Bari) Various €22-€75
Naples → Lecce Frecciarossa 5h 30-6h 0-1 Various €25-€80

Key Decision: Intercity (IC) is the clear winner — direct service, First Class available, and no slow regional connections required.

Is It a Milk Run? First Class Options

Short answer: Take the Intercity (IC) train — it’s NOT a milk run and has First Class seating.

Option Type Duration Class Verdict
Intercity (IC) Express conventional ~5h 30m 1st + 2nd Class ✅ Recommended
Frecciarossa to Bari + Regional High-speed + slow regional 5–6h Business on FS leg only Regional leg IS slow (many stops)

Why Intercity (IC) is the right choice: - Runs the full Naples → Brindisi route without changing trains - Makes major stops only (Foggia, Bari, coastal towns) — not every tiny station - First Class on IC trains: reserved seat, more space, dedicated car, quieter - IC trains are not Frecciarossa-class but they are proper express trains - Fares: €15–40 First Class depending on advance booking - Book at trenitalia.com — search Naples Centrale → Brindisi, filter by IC

What makes the “milk run” concern valid: The Bari→Brindisi regional (Regionale) connection, if you take the Frecciarossa-then-change-at-Bari approach, IS slow — it stops at many coastal towns. That’s the connection to avoid. The IC bypasses this entirely.

Booking tip: IC trains must be booked on Trenitalia (Italo doesn’t serve this southern route). Book 2–4 weeks ahead for best fares and seat selection.

Understanding Puglia Geography

Important: Brindisi is IN Puglia, not before it. The train journey IS a tour through Puglia:

Route Path (North to South): 1. Caserta - Royal Palace (Reggia di Caserta), visible from station 2. Benevento - Roman Arch of Trajan 3. Foggia - Enter Puglia here (gateway to Gargano Peninsula) 4. Bari - Puglia’s capital (~3.5h from Naples) 5. Polignano a Mare - Dramatic cliffside town 6. Ostuni - “White City” on hilltop 7. Brindisi - Historic port city (~5.5h from Naples) 8. Lecce - “Florence of the South” (~6h from Naples, 30 min beyond Brindisi)

Recommended Train

Intercity (IC) Naples Centrale → Brindisi - Morning departure recommended (arrive Brindisi early afternoon) - Check current schedules at trenitalia.com — schedules change seasonally - First Class car: look for “Prima Classe” designation when booking - Journey time: approximately 5h 30min direct

Departure Times: - Morning Options: Earliest ~8:12 AM, Latest ~6:12 PM - Strategy: Take morning train to maximize daylight views through Puglia

Scenic Journey Highlights

What You’ll See: - Crossing the Apennines (mountains) - Entering the Tavoliere delle Puglie (plains) - Adriatic coastline (sit on LEFT side after Foggia) - Olive groves stretching to horizon - Whitewashed towns on hillsides

Recommended Approach

Option A: Direct to Brindisi (Simple) - Morning Frecce train from Naples - Arrive Brindisi early afternoon (~5.5 hours) - Meet tour group or check into hotel - One journey, minimal hassle

Option B: Stop in Bari (Recommended if Time Allows) - Take morning train to Bari (3.5h) - Spend afternoon/evening in Bari Vecchia (old town) - Watch orecchiette ladies making pasta - Visit Basilica di San Nicola - Fresh seafood dinner along the port - Next morning: Easy 1-1.5 hour regional train to Brindisi

Bari Benefits: - Breaks up long journey - Taste of Puglia before tour begins - Beautiful old town (Bari Vecchia) - Day trip options: Matera (cave dwellings, 1h), Alberobello (trulli houses, 1.5h), Polignano a Mare (cliffs, 30 min)

Regional Connections Within Puglia

Once in Puglia, regional trains connect key towns:

Route Duration Frequency Price
Bari → Brindisi 1-1.5h Frequent €8-€15
Bari → Lecce 1.5h Hourly €12-€25
Brindisi → Lecce 30 min Frequent €5-€10

General Train Travel Tips

Booking Strategy

When to Book: - High-speed trains: 2-4 weeks ahead for Super Economy fares - Regional trains: Can buy same day (no reservations)

Where to Book: - Trenitalia.com or Italotreno.it (direct booking best) - Omio, Rail Europe (third-party, often higher fees) - Station ticket windows (if you prefer in-person)

At the Station

Arrival Time: - High-speed trains: Arrive 15-20 minutes early - Regional trains: Arrive 10 minutes early - Check platform (binario) on departure boards

Validating Tickets: - E-tickets (QR code): No validation needed, show on phone - Paper tickets: MUST validate in yellow machines before boarding - Failure to validate = fine from conductor

On the Train

Luggage: - Store large bags in designated racks (near doors or overhead) - Keep valuables with you - Watch bags during stops

Seats: - High-speed: Assigned seats (on ticket) - Regional: Open seating (first-come, first-served)

Quiet Cars: - Some trains have “Silenzio” cars (no phone calls, quiet conversations)

Train Classes Compared

Trenitalia Classes: - Standard/2nd Class: Comfortable, affordable - Premium: More space, quieter - Business: Leather seats, welcome drink, snacks - Executive: Most spacious, full meal service (rare on our routes)

Italo Classes: - Smart: Economy - Comfort: Business equivalent - Prima: First class equivalent

Station Information

Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN)

Address: Piazza della Stazione, 50123 Firenze Key Features: - Central location (walk to Duomo in 10 min) - Luggage storage (€6-€12 per bag per day) - Pharmacy, cafes, shops - Free WiFi

Getting to Hotels: - Many Florence hotels walkable - Taxi stand outside main entrance - Trams and buses available

Napoli Centrale

Address: Piazza Garibaldi, 80142 Napoli Key Features: - Main Naples station (also called “Garibaldi” due to piazza location) - Can be crowded - watch belongings - Metro connections (Line 1 and 2) - Luggage storage available - Taxi stand (use official white taxis only)

Safety Note: - Busy station attracts pickpockets - Keep bags close and zipped - Use official taxis (white with meters)

Bari Centrale

Address: Piazza Aldo Moro, 70122 Bari Key Features: - Modern, clean station - Luggage storage - 15-20 min walk to Bari Vecchia (old town) - Good cafes and restaurants nearby

Brindisi Stazione

Address: Piazza Vittorio Emanuele III, 72100 Brindisi Key Features: - Smaller station - 10 min walk to historic center - Taxi stand available - Ferry terminal to Greece nearby (we won’t use)

Luggage Storage

Available at: - Firenze SMN: Yes (€6-€12/bag/day) - Napoli Centrale: Yes - Bari Centrale: Yes - Brindisi: Limited

Useful For: - Day trips from base city - Early hotel checkout but late train - Exploring city upon arrival before hotel check-in

Important Links

Booking

  • Trenitalia
  • Italo
  • Omio (multi-modal search)

Station Info

  • Firenze SMN
  • Napoli Centrale

Apps

  • Trenitalia App (iOS/Android) - Book, manage tickets, live updates
  • Italo Treno App - Same for Italo trains
  • Google Maps - Good for finding platforms and connections
